primary_sources.title: Decree of the Sultan of Selangor on pig farming, formally adopted by Mesyuarat Majlis Mesyuarat Kerajaan Negeri (MMKN — Selangor Exco) 12 February 2026
Selangor State Constitution Article 50 (executive authority vested in Sultan, exercisable on Executive Council advice)
Federal Constitution Article 71 and Eighth Schedule (requiring 'on advice' provision in every state constitution)

intervention_outcome: Small-scale non-Muslim pig-farming operators in Tanjong Sepat face phased closure; licensing had stopped since January 2026. The 16 May Daulat Tuanku rally backed the institution, with about 307 police reports lodged against a caricature deemed insulting to Sultan Sharafuddin. DAP reps Wong Siew Ki and Ronnie Liu defended the pig farmers under Federal Constitution Article 8(2) and became the focal point of the counter-mobilisation.
notes: The Sultan-in-Council route is constitutional. The royal decree became Selangor state policy through Article 50 (Sultan-in-Council) plus the 12 February Exco adoption. The downstream proposal — Ibrahim Ali's Akta Kedaulatan Raja with 14 days' detention without warrant plus preventive detention without trial for speech about royalty — would revive a category Parliament repealed in 2012 (ISA). Encoded as RoyalIntervention via state-constitutional mechanism. Federal-level legislative consequences are separate (latent LegislativeVote candidates).
